meta_title: Impact Doors in Davie, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Davie, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph rated, licensed & insured. Free estimates — financing from $87/mo.
h1: Davie's Premier Choice for Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors That Protect What Matters Most
intro:
Davie, Florida is one of Broward County's most distinctive communities — a rare blend of suburban comfort, equestrian character, and genuine neighborhood pride stretching across zip codes from 33314 to 33332. From the sprawling ranches of Long Lake Ranches and Rolling Hills to the established family streets of Shenandoah and Arrowhead, homeowners here have invested deeply in their properties. With an average home value of $460,000 and rising, protecting that investment against South Florida's relentless storm season isn't optional — it's essential. Impact doors represent one of the single most important upgrades a Davie homeowner can make, providing a critical line of defense that standard doors simply cannot offer.

While Davie sits approximately eight miles from the Atlantic coastline, that distance provides little comfort when a major hurricane makes landfall nearby. The town sits squarely in a 170-mph wind zone under Florida Building Code requirements, meaning every exterior door must meet strict performance standards to keep your home and family safe. Unlike communities that fall within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) of Miami-Dade and certain coastal Broward areas, Davie homes are not automatically subject to the most stringent HVHZ product approvals — but Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated products for all new installations and replacements. For the roughly 40% of Davie homes built before 1994 — before modern hurricane-resistant construction standards were adopted — the urgency of upgrading to impact doors is even greater.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of homeowners across Davie and throughout Broward County make the transition from outdated, vulnerable entry points to high-performance impact doors that stand up to whatever the Atlantic hurricane season delivers. Whether you're updating a grand double-door entry in Ivanhoe, replacing sliding glass doors overlooking a Rolling Hills backyard, or installing elegant French doors in a Long Lake Ranches estate home, our team brings the experience, craftsmanship, and locally-sourced knowledge to get the job done right — completely in-house, with no subcontractors, and no shortcuts.

section_why.h2: Why Davie Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Davie isn't just about finding the lowest bid — it's about finding a team with the depth of expertise, local regulatory knowledge, and proven track record to protect a home worth nearly half a million dollars. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every project, and we've built our reputation as 'The Kings of Service' not through marketing slogans but through consistent results across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Our A+ BBB rating and 500+ completed homes speak to a standard of quality that Davie homeowners can trust.

One of the most important distinctions between Premier Impact Windows & Roofing and many of our competitors is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our work to third-party crews whose quality we cannot guarantee. Every impact door we install in Davie — from a single fiberglass entry door on a Shenandoah cul-de-sac to a full set of impact sliding glass doors on a Long Lake Ranches estate — is handled by our own trained, licensed, and insured installation teams. This means direct accountability at every step, proper handling of product warranties, and the assurance that your installation will pass Town of Davie Building Division inspections the first time.

We also understand the specific character of Davie's housing stock. With 40% of homes predating 1994, many properties feature original door frames, non-standard rough openings, and older construction methods that require a knowledgeable hand during installation. Our crews are experienced in working with older Davie homes — whether that means custom framing solutions, addressing wood rot discovered during installation, or navigating the permit process with the Town of Davie Building Division on your behalf. We handle the permitting paperwork so you don't have to.

Financing is available starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it genuinely accessible for Davie families to upgrade without draining savings or delaying the protection their home needs. We work with multiple lending partners to find a payment structure that fits your budget, so there's no reason to put off an investment that immediately begins paying dividends in security, energy efficiency, and insurance savings.

section_history.h2: Lessons From the Storm: How Davie's Hurricane History Makes the Case for Impact Doors
section_history.body:
Davie has faced the full force of South Florida's storm history in a way that few inland communities can claim. Since 1990, the area has experienced nine significant storm events, three of which left lasting marks on the community's collective memory and building stock. Hurricane Andrew in August 1992 — the catastrophic Category 5 storm that reshaped Florida's entire approach to building codes — cut a swath through South Florida that extended well into Broward County. Though Andrew's eye tracked south through Miami-Dade, its outer bands delivered punishing wind speeds to Davie, exposing the catastrophic vulnerability of pre-code construction. Thousands of homes lost entry doors, garage doors, and sliding glass doors entirely, allowing wind pressure to build inside structures and leading to catastrophic roof failures.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 brought a different but equally sobering lesson. As a powerful Category 3 storm at landfall, Wilma moved rapidly across the Florida peninsula, striking Broward County with sustained winds exceeding 120 mph and gusts that surpassed those figures significantly. Davie neighborhoods including Arrowhead and Rolling Hills experienced widespread damage to doors and windows, and the storm vividly demonstrated that interior Broward communities are not sheltered from major storm impacts by their distance from the coast. Hurricane Irma in 2017, though weakened somewhat by the time it reached Broward, still produced tropical-storm-to-hurricane-force gusts across Davie, causing additional door and window failures in homes that had still not been upgraded in the years since Wilma.

The pattern is clear: Davie homeowners who rely on standard hollow-core or even solid-wood entry doors, unlaminated sliding glass doors, or older French door assemblies without multi-point locking systems are gambling with their greatest financial asset every hurricane season. Impact doors are not a luxury upgrade — they are an evidence-based response to a documented, recurring threat that has visited this community nine times in three decades and will return again.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance and the Davie Permit Process for Impact Door Installation
section_code.body:
Impact door installation in Davie is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), which sets performance standards that all replacement and new-construction exterior doors must meet throughout Broward County. Although Davie does not fall within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade County and certain coastal areas — this does not mean homeowners have a lower standard to meet. Broward County properties in Davie's wind zone must still install products rated to withstand the area's design wind speeds of 170 mph, and all impact doors must carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers demonstrating they have been tested and certified to meet FBC requirements for large missile impact, cyclic wind pressure, and water infiltration.

Every impact door installation in Davie requires a building permit pulled through the Town of Davie Building Division. This is not merely a bureaucratic formality — the permit process ensures that your installation is inspected by a licensed building official who verifies that the product, the framing, the installation method, and the fastening pattern all meet code. Unpermitted impact door installations can create serious problems at resale, may void manufacturer warranties, and — critically — may not be recognized by your insurance carrier when you submit a wind mitigation inspection report to qualify for premium discounts. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application process on your behalf, coordinating directly with the Town of Davie Building Division from submission through final inspection sign-off.

For homeowners in pre-1994 Davie homes, there is an additional consideration: older door frames may not meet current rough opening requirements or may have deteriorated in ways that require supplemental work to bring the installation into compliance. Our team performs a thorough pre-installation assessment of every opening to identify any framing or structural issues before work begins, ensuring that your final installation is code-compliant, properly inspected, and fully documented for insurance and resale purposes.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Broward County's Toughest Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors exclusively from two industry-leading brands — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — chosen specifically for their performance credentials, aesthetic range, and proven reliability in South Florida's demanding climate. Both brands carry comprehensive Florida Product Approvals and are engineered to meet the wind load and impact resistance requirements of Broward County's 170-mph wind zone, giving Davie homeowners confidence that their doors will perform when it matters most.

ESW impact doors are engineered with heavy-duty aluminum or composite frames and incorporate laminated safety glass where vision panels are present — the same bonded PVB interlayer technology used in automotive windshields. Even under direct large-missile impact, the glass holds together rather than shattering into dangerous projectiles. ESW's entry door systems, sliding glass doors, and French door configurations all feature multi-point locking mechanisms that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, providing vastly superior wind resistance compared to traditional single-point deadbolt systems. For Davie homeowners in neighborhoods like Long Lake Ranches or Ivanhoe where aesthetics matter as much as performance, ESW offers a wide range of panel designs, glass configurations, hardware finishes, and color options to complement the architectural character of your home.

CWI impact door products bring an equally strong performance pedigree with a focus on energy efficiency alongside hurricane protection. CWI doors are designed to meet or exceed ENERGY STAR standards, incorporating thermally broken frames and low-E glass options that reduce solar heat gain and keep conditioned air inside your home. For Davie homeowners running central air conditioning through South Florida's long summer season, this translates to measurable reductions in monthly utility costs. CWI's sliding glass door systems are particularly well-suited for the larger openings common in Davie's pool homes and estate properties, offering wide panel configurations without sacrificing the structural integrity required for hurricane certification.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Davie — and Why the Investment Pays for Itself
section_cost.body:
For Davie homeowners with properties averaging $460,000 in value, impact door installation represents a targeted, high-return investment rather than a discretionary expense. The cost of impact door installation in Davie varies based on door type, size, configuration, and the condition of existing framing, but as a general guide: a single impact entry door installation typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 installed; impact sliding glass doors range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more depending on panel width and configuration; and impact French door systems are priced similarly, depending on size and glass package. Homes requiring multiple door replacements — a common scenario in older Davie properties with original 1980s or early-1990s door packages — can expect total project costs in the $8,000 to $20,000 range for a comprehensive upgrade.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it possible for Davie families to move forward immediately without depleting emergency savings or waiting through another hurricane season with vulnerable doors in place. Our financing partners offer competitive rates and flexible terms, and our team will walk you through available options during your free in-home estimate so you can make a fully informed decision. Many homeowners find that when they factor in insurance premium savings, the net monthly cost of financing their impact door upgrade is significantly offset from day one.

The return on investment for impact doors in Davie is compelling on multiple fronts. Beyond the immediate security and storm protection benefits, impact doors add directly to appraised home value — a meaningful consideration in a market where the average Davie home is worth $460,000 and climbing. Real estate professionals consistently report that impact-rated openings are a sought-after feature by buyers in Broward County, often accelerating sale timelines and supporting stronger offers. Combined with energy savings, insurance discounts, and the elimination of annual hurricane shutter installation costs, impact doors typically deliver full return on investment within three to seven years.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Real Insurance Savings: How Impact Doors Reduce Your Broward County Home Insurance Premium
section_insurance.body:
One of the most tangible and immediate financial benefits of installing impact doors in Davie is the reduction in your homeowner's insurance premium. Florida's insurance market has experienced dramatic rate increases in recent years, with Broward County homeowners among the hardest hit. However, the Florida Building Code and the state's wind mitigation credit system create a direct financial incentive for homeowners who invest in hurricane-rated openings — and impact doors are among the highest-value improvements you can make to your wind mitigation profile.

After your impact door installation is complete and your building permit is closed out by the Town of Davie Building Division, you are eligible to request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector. This inspection documents the hurricane protection features of your home — including impact-rated doors — and produces a standardized report that you submit to your insurance carrier. Under Florida law, carriers are required to offer actuarially justified discounts for homes with verified wind mitigation features. In practical terms, Davie homeowners who upgrade all exterior openings to impact-rated products — doors and windows combined — often see annual premium reductions of 20% to 45%, depending on their current carrier and policy structure. On a Broward County home insurance policy that may be running $5,000 to $10,000 or more annually, those savings are substantial.

Major carriers active in the Davie market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — all recognize wind mitigation credits for properly installed, permitted, and inspected impact doors. It is important that your installation is fully permitted and inspected, as carriers will not apply discounts for unpermitted work — another reason why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's full-service approach, including handling all permitting through the Town of Davie Building Division, is so valuable. We also recommend that homeowners contact their insurance agent immediately after receiving their wind mitigation report, as credits are applied from the date the report is submitted — not retroactively.

faqs.q: Do impact doors in Davie need to be rated for the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ)?
faqs.a: Davie is not located within the HVHZ, which primarily covers Miami-Dade County and certain coastal areas. However, Davie falls within a 170-mph wind zone under Florida Building Code, and all impact doors installed here must carry valid Florida Product Approvals demonstrating they meet the wind load and impact resistance requirements for Broward County. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs ESW and CWI doors that carry appropriate FBC approvals for Davie's specific wind zone requirements.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost for a Davie home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Davie vary by door type and configuration. A single entry door installation generally runs $1,500–$3,500 installed; sliding glass doors range from $2,500–$6,000+; and full-home door packages can range from $8,000–$20,000 depending on the scope. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options through multiple lending partners. We provide free in-home estimates so you receive an accurate, no-obligation quote specific to your home before committing to anything.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Davie, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes — all impact door installations in Davie require a building permit from the Town of Davie Building Division. The permit process ensures your installation is inspected and code-compliant, which is essential for insurance wind mitigation credits and protecting your home's resale value. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit application and inspection coordination process on your behalf, from initial submission through final sign-off, so you don't have to navigate the Town of Davie Building Division on your own.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Davie home from start to finish?
faqs.a: The typical timeline from signed contract to completed installation for a Davie impact door project is four to eight weeks. This includes the time required to pull permits from the Town of Davie Building Division, order your specific door configurations from ESW or CWI, and schedule installation with our in-house crews. The physical installation itself is generally completed in one to two days depending on the number of doors being replaced. We keep you updated throughout the process and coordinate the final building inspection on your behalf.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact doors in Davie?
faqs.a: Davie homeowners who install impact-rated doors and windows and obtain a wind mitigation inspection report typically see annual insurance premium reductions of 20% to 45%, depending on their carrier and the extent of their upgrade. On a Broward County policy running $5,000–$10,000 annually, that translates to $1,000–$4,500 or more in yearly savings. Impact doors also add to your home's appraised value and eliminate the cost and hassle of installing hurricane shutters each season, further improving the overall return on your investment.
